name: Liliha
desc: Old-school local neighborhood — Liliha Bakery territory, diverse, affordable, transitional feel between Nuuanu and Kalihi.
insider: Liliha Bakery coco puffs (chantilly cream-filled puffs) are legendary — go before 8am or hit the Nimitz Hwy location for less wait
The neighborhood is genuinely local — you'll hear more pidgin than English on any given block
Liliha Street is one-way in sections during rush hour — check the signs carefully or you'll get honked at and ticketed
It's one of the most affordable walkable neighborhoods with real character left in metro Honolulu
Meg's Drive-In on Liliha Street is an old-school plate lunch institution — the fried chicken is excellent
Kunawai Spring (near Kunawai Lane) is an ancient Hawaiian freshwater spring considered sacred — treat it with respect
The old Liliha homes have genuine plantation-era charm — wooden cottages with louvered windows and covered lanais
Fort Shafter military base borders the neighborhood — you'll see uniformed personnel at the local shops regularly
Street crime is occasional — keep cars locked and don't leave valuables visible, standard urban Honolulu caution
The Nuuanu Stream walkway connects Liliha to Chinatown on foot — a nice urban walk but skip it after dark
